checks.a_analysis: The output text across all sections was compared to the source PDF. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were found. The content of the tables, specifically in section 14 and the settings sections, accurately reflects the visual information presented in the corresponding screenshots within the source document. The use of 'null' and 'false' in form-related tables is a standard artifact of representing interactive elements in a static JSON structure.

The contact information on the final page was verified. The name 'Ablr' and the phone number '833-294-2257' match the source document exactly. No fabricated or mismatched URLs, emails, or addresses were found.

While the provided JSON uses an omission placeholder for several sections, it explicitly accounts for all 34 pages of the source document either through direct conversion or marked omission. No page or section appears to have been lost entirely during the conversion process.

The tables in the output representing forms and settings (sections 6, 7, 9, 10, 14, 17, 19, 20) were compared against the visual data in the source PDF. All tables contain the correct number of rows corresponding to the fields and properties shown in the source images.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: In the final section 'Get in touch' (Page 34), two critical pieces of contact information are missing: the website URL ('Web: www.ablr360.com') and the email address ('Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com'). This represents an omission of 2 out of 6 main text elements in that section (33.3%), which exceeds the 25% threshold for a major defect.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording of all converted text sections was reviewed. No instances of altered meaning or incorrect numbers/dates were found beyond minor whitespace normalization.

checks.a_analysis: The document structure was examined for any repeated text blocks or sections that appear once in the source but multiple times in the output. The 'Ablr' logo appears on multiple pages in the source (1, 3, 13, 34) and is appropriately reflected in the output at corresponding reading order positions. No instances of unintentional duplication of text or structural elements were found.

The heading hierarchy is consistent throughout the document. Each slide title is assigned an H1 level, which is a common and acceptable normalization for presentation slides to ensure accessibility. There are no skipped levels (e.g., H1 to H3) and the nesting is logical.

The pipeline has intelligently converted several form-like images into structured tables for better accessibility. For instance, the 'Billing Contact' form on page 6, the 'Voter Registration Form' on page 7, and various software settings dialogs are all represented as tables with clear row and column structures. These tables accurately reflect the information shown in the source images without corruption of columns, rows, or headers.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the source document. Within each section (slide), the heading is followed by the relevant text, lists, tables, and finally images. On multi-column slides like page 34, the reading order correctly processes elements from top-to-bottom and left-to-right (Get in touch -> Thank You! -> contact details -> QR code).

Several numbered lists in the source PDF have been converted into unordered lists in the output. Specifically, the lists on pages 6, 7, 9, and 10 use numbers (1, 2, 3...) in the source, but the JSON defines them as 'unordered' and omits the numbers from the text content. This changes the structural meaning of the list from an ordered sequence to a simple set of items.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 6: '1. Use underline', '2. Can use...'; Page 7: '1. Enter field name', '2. Select...', '3. Add...', '4. Save...'; Page 9: '1. Add radio buttons', '2. Select...', '3. Enter...', '4. Enter...', '5. Enter...'; Page 10: '1. Use Preview...', '2. To clear...', '3. Select Clear...'
checks.b_evidence_output: Reading Order 24 (Page 6), 32 (Page 7), 41 (Page 9), 46 (Page 10): all lists marked as 'list_type': 'unordered' with numbers removed from text.

checks.a_analysis: On page 34, the contact information includes a website and an email address that are clearly visible and formatted as links in the source PDF. These two elements (and their associated URLs) are entirely missing from the output text and structure, while the phone number is preserved. This constitutes a failure to preserve visible links.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text elements and links for the website and email are absent between 'Ablr' (index 165) and 'Call: 833-294-2257' (index 167).

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output linearizes the presentation slides into a single scrolling page. The hierarchy of information, established through headings and bulleted lists, remains consistent with the source PDF. While many images are missing (evaluated in V3), the overall visual organization of the remaining text and tables follows the flow of the original document.

The conversion team has rendered numerous screenshots of forms and settings dialogs as HTML tables. These tables are visually sound, with clear row/column structures and legible headers that match the data presented in the source images. Examples include the 'Billing Contact' form on page 6 and 'NVDA Settings' on page 19.

A significant number of meaningful instructional images from the source PDF are missing in the rendered HTML. While some screenshots have been replaced by tables, many others—such as keyboard photos, software menus, and specific UI panels—are entirely absent. This includes critical instructional visuals like the Acrobat Tags panel (p.11), keyboard shortcut properties (p.15), keyboard photos for NVDA and VoiceOver (p.16, 18, 26, 27, 28), and the QR code on the final slide (p.34).
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains instructional screenshots on pages 10, 11, 12, 15, 21, 25, 29, 30, and 32, as well as keyboard photos on pages 16, 18, 26, 27, and 28, and a QR code on page 34.
checks.b_evidence_output: The corresponding sections in the HTML output (e.g., 'Form Tags in Adobe Acrobat', 'Creating a Keyboard Shortcut', 'VoiceOver Resources', etc.) are missing these images.
checks.b_evidence_location: Multiple sections throughout the document, specifically slides 10-12, 15, 16, 18, 21, 25-30, 32, and 34.

checks.a_analysis: No visual duplication of body content was observed. The Table of Contents links in the sidebar are a standard navigation feature and do not constitute content duplication. The conversion of screenshot data into accessible tables (e.g., Slide 6 and 7) is a remediation step, not duplication.
